questions.question: Describe the main difference between a Deterministic Finite Automaton (DFA) and a Nondeterministic Finite Automaton (NFA). Provide an example to illustrate your answer.
questions.hint: Focus on the key characteristics of each type

questions.question: Design a DFA to recognize the pattern 'abc' in a given string. Be sure to account for the possibility of a string not containing the pattern. Provide a clear diagram illustrating your DFA's transition function.
questions.hint: Use a visual aid to help with your diagram

questions.question: Consider an NFA that recognizes the pattern 'ab*'. How can you modify the NFA to create an equivalent DFA? Explain your reasoning and provide a step-by-step guide to the transformation.
questions.hint: Focus on the epsilon-transitions and how they can be handled in a DFA
